records.ety: [OE. blason, blasoun, shield, fr. F. blason coat of arms, OF. shield, from the root of AS. blæse blaze, i. e., luster, splendor, MHG. blas torch See Blaze, n.]

defs: An heraldic shield; a coat of arms, or a bearing on a coat of arms; armorial bearings.
quotes: Their blazon o'er his towers displayed.

defs: The art or act of describing or depicting heraldic bearings in the proper language or manner.
Ostentatious display, either by words or other means; publication; show; description; record.
quotes: Obtrude the blazon of their exploits upon the company.
Thy tongue, thy face, thy limbs, actions, and spirit, Do give thee fivefold blazon.

records.ety: [From blazon, n.; confused with 4th blaze: cf. F. blasonner.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Blazoned (<?/); p. pr. & vb. n. Blazoning (<?/).]

defs: To depict in colors; to display; to exhibit conspicuously; to publish or make public far and wide.
quotes: There pride sits blazoned on th' unmeaning brow.
To blazon his own worthless name.

defs: To deck; to embellish; to adorn.
quotes: She blazons in dread smiles her hideous form.

defs: To describe in proper terms (the figures of heraldic devices); also, to delineate (armorial bearings); to emblazon.
quotes: The coat of , arms, which I am not herald enough to blazon into English.
